page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the N1 server family. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is configured with 30 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate a moderate multi-core profile alongside weaker single-core capabilities. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the bottom 25% and 10% respectively, reflecting the older CPU generation. However, multi-core stress-ng performance and PassMark CPU scores reach average levels. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks consistently register average performance, with read-only large block bandwidth at 45.37 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 34.65 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the n1-standard-8 is its lower single-threaded processing speed, as evidenced by poor performance in gzip compression and text processing. Despite these single-core limitations, the instance maintains average capabilities in database operations and multi-core tasks. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as moderate web serving, basic database operations, and multi-threaded utility tasks that do not require high-frequency single-core execution or local NVMe storage.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server from the N1 family, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor at 2.3 GHz, paired with 30 GB of RAM. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff: single-core performance is weak to poor, as shown by low Geekbench and compression scores, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. Specifically, PassMark database operations and multi-core stress-ng tests perform at average levels, whereas Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput are weaker. This instance is best suited for balanced, multi-threaded workloads such as moderate database operations, basic web serving, and general-purpose utility tasks that do not depend on high single-threaded CPU speeds.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 8 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering balanced multi-core performance for standard workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is a general-purpose server with 8 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, suited for balanced multi-core tasks.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and moderate multi-core performance for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs and 30 GB of system memory
Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
No local storage or GPU acceleration included
Best suited for moderate database operations and general-purpose web serving
